subject: Social Science (Economics)
overall_learning_objectives: Critically analyse interdependence among sectors of the economy
Interpret GDP and employment data trends
Evaluate problems like disguised and underemployment
Compare organised and unorganised sectors analytically
Examine role of public and private sectors in development
Develop data interpretation and case-based reasoning skills

lesson_plan.title: Deep Analysis of Economic Sectors
teaching_strategy: Case study: From raw cotton to export garment
student_activities: Identify how each sector supports the other

lesson_plan.title: GDP Contribution vs Employment
student_activities: Explain why primary sector employs more but contributes less

lesson_plan.title: Disguised and Underemployment – Critical View
student_activities: Analyse case and suggest employment solutions
Write short explanation on productivity loss

lesson_plan.title: Organised vs Unorganised Sector – Analytical Comparison
teaching_strategy: Case study (factory worker vs street vendor)
student_activities: Debate: Which sector needs more government support?

lesson_plan.title: Public and Private Sectors – Role in Development
teaching_strategy: Discussion: Indian Railways vs Private Airlines
student_activities: Analyse benefits of public sector in rural areas

lesson_plan.title: Sector Growth and Sustainable Development
student_activities: Suggest policies to improve employment in secondary sector

lesson_plan.title: Integrated Case-Based Assessment and Revision
student_activities: Write short answer: 'Why is service sector growth not enough for employment?'

teacher_tip: Focus on connecting data with concepts. Encourage students to justify answers using economic reasoning rather than memorised definitions.
